content.problemStatement:
You are given nnn sets S1,S2,…,SnS_1, S_2, \ldots, S_nS1​,S2​,…,Sn​.

For each iii, you want to choose xi∈Six_i \in S_ixi​∈Si​, such that the variance of the  multiset {x1,x2,…,xn}\{x_1, x_2, \ldots, x_n\}{x1​,x2​,…,xn​} is minimized.

The variance of a multiset {x1,x2,…,xn}\{x_1, x_2, \ldots, x_n\}{x1​,x2​,…,xn​} is defined as σ2=∑i=1n(xi−μ)2n\sigma^2 = \displaystyle  \dfrac{\sum_{i=1}^{n} (x_i - \mu)^2}{n} σ2=n∑i=1n​(xi​−μ)2​, where μ\muμ is the mean, which equals ∑i=1nxin\displaystyle \dfrac{ \sum_{i=1}^{n}x_i}{n}n∑i=1n​xi​​.

Say the minimum possible variance is RRR. Print n2×Rn^2 \times Rn2×R. It can be proved that n2Rn^2 Rn2R is always an integer.
constraints: All elements in a single set are distinct.
The elements of all the sets are between 111 and 50,00050,00050,000 inclusive
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains nnn, the number of sets.
ithi^{\text{th}} ith of the next nnn lines contain an integer ∣Si∣|S_i|∣Si​∣ followed by ∣Si∣|S_i|∣Si​∣ distinct integers denoting the elements of set SiS_iSi​.
content.outputFormat: In the only line, print a single integer denoting the product of n2n^2n2 and the minimum possible variance of {x1,x2,…,xn}\{x_1, x_2, \ldots, x_n\}{x1​,x2​,…,xn​}

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: You should choose x1=3x_1 = 3x1​=3 from S1S_1S1​ and x2=5x_2 = 5x2​=5 from S2S_2S2​, the mean is 444 and the variance is (3−4)2+(5−4)22=1\dfrac{(3-4)^2 + (5 - 4)^2}{2} = 12(3−4)2+(5−4)2​=1. Hence, the output should be n2×1=4n^2 \times 1 = 4n2×1=4.
